admission_hnp.hpi: Patient arrived in acute respiratory distress with hypoxemia requiring emergent intubation. Hospital course complicated by atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response and concern for hospital-acquired infection later in stay. Comorbid COPD, CKD stage 3, diabetes with neuropathy, and protein-calorie malnutrition increased risk and complexity.
past_medical_history: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with peripheral neuropathy
medications: Insulin lispro sliding scale with meals

progress_notes.title: MICU Admission Note
progress_notes.author: Priya Raman, MD
events: Intubated for acute respiratory failure with severe hypoxemia.
Initial hypotension after sedation; improved with fluid and vasopressor bolus.
assessment: Respiratory failure requiring invasive mechanical ventilation.
COPD exacerbation with significant work of breathing.
plan: Lung-protective ventilation strategy and serial ABGs.

progress_notes.title: Ventilator Management Note
progress_notes.author: Priya Raman, MD
events: Mechanical ventilation ongoing; cumulative duration 24 hours.
Lactate remained elevated; creatinine increased above baseline CKD stage 3.
assessment: Critical illness with multiorgan stress pattern.
plan: Continue ventilator support and spontaneous breathing trial readiness checks.
Early enteral nutrition and electrolyte optimization.

progress_notes.title: Extubation Progress Note
progress_notes.author: Priya Raman, MD
events: Successfully extubated after total 36 hours mechanical ventilation.
Oxygen requirement decreased to 3 L nasal cannula.
assessment: Hemodynamics stable without sustained pressor infusion.
plan: Transfer from MICU to telemetry when stable for 12 hours.
Advance diet with nutrition consult follow-up.

progress_notes.title: Telemetry Escalation Note
progress_notes.author: Nina Patel, MD
events: New atrial fibrillation with RVR to 150 bpm; treated with diltiazem.
Mild hypotension and lactate rise prompted rapid-response assessment.
assessment: Need to monitor for infection-related trigger.
plan: Rate control and anticoagulation risk discussion.
Continue broad-spectrum coverage pending source clarification.

progress_notes.title: Possible HAI Note
progress_notes.author: Nina Patel, MD
events: Fever and rising WBC with concern for hospital-acquired infection.
Documentation noted possible HAI but source remained unclear in note.
assessment: High-risk case with unresolved infection source linkage.
Respiratory status stable on low-flow oxygen.
plan: Targeted imaging and sputum culture review.
Continue antibiotics with de-escalation strategy once source identified.
Monitor renal function and drug dosing with CKD stage 3.

progress_notes.title: Improvement Note
progress_notes.author: Nina Patel, MD
events: No recurrent AF with RVR in last 48 hours.
WBC, lactate, and oxygen requirement improved.
assessment: Overall clinical recovery with residual deconditioning.
Appropriate for discharge planning and close outpatient follow-up.
plan: Discharge with pulmonary and cardiology appointments.
Document clear return precautions and medication reconciliation.
Nutrition support plan and diabetes regimen review.

escalation_event.pressor_use: Intermittent norepinephrine bolus support, no prolonged drip
escalation_event.outcome: Extubated and stepped down to telemetry
transfer_note: Transferred to MICU after emergency intubation for respiratory failure.
Ventilation continued for 36 hours with improvement in gas exchange.
Step-down completed after extubation and stable hemodynamics.

lab_interpretation: Abnormal trend present: persistent leukocytosis with secondary spike during possible HAI period.
Lactate remained elevated early then normalized with respiratory/hemodynamic improvement.
Creatinine stayed above baseline CKD stage 3 range throughout stay.

procedure_report.indication: Acute respiratory failure with concern for infectious etiology
findings: Diffuse erythematous airways with moderate purulent secretions.
BAL samples collected from right middle lobe for culture and cytology.
No endobronchial mass or active bleeding seen.

final_diagnoses: Acute respiratory failure requiring 36-hour mechanical ventilation
Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response, resolved
Possible hospital-acquired infection (source not clearly linked)
hospital_course: Required ICU admission and ventilatory support for high-acuity respiratory decompensation.
Extubated successfully after 36 hours with gradual oxygen wean.
Developed AF with RVR and possible HAI event before eventual stabilization.
follow_up: Pulmonology follow-up in 1 week with repeat chest imaging.
Cardiology follow-up in 2 weeks for AF monitoring.
Nephrology and nutrition follow-up within 2-3 weeks.
discharge_summary.author: Priya Raman, MD

secondary_diagnoses: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease with exacerbation
Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic neuropathy
Severe systemic response without shock, monitored
documentation_gaps: Respiratory failure documented without type qualifier
Clarify hypoxic vs hypercapnic vs both
Hospital-acquired infection noted without linked source
Specify source: pneumonia, line, urinary, or other
